turns.content: right so I'm trying to coordinate an Eid gathering for my whole family and it's like herding cats wallah
Eid planning with a big family can be chaotic — how many people are we talking about, and what's the main thing you need help with?
so it's my parents, me and my siblings, plus a few aunties and uncles and cousins. easily 25-30 people. and everyone has opinions about everything but nobody wants to actually DO anything

Classic. The "everyone has preferences but no one volunteers" dynamic. Are you hosting at your parents' place or are you trying to find a venue? And are you the one who's somehow ended up in charge of organising?

my parents' house yeah. and obviously I'm in charge because I'm the oldest init so apparently that means I'm the event planner. my siblings are being useless about it. I made a group chat specifically for planning and half of them haven't even responded

The dedicated planning group chat that gets ignored is a universal experience. Have you tried assigning specific tasks to specific people instead of asking for general help? People respond better to "you're bringing dessert" than "who can bring dessert?"

that's literally what I need to do. I just didn't want to be bossy about it but clearly the polite approach isn't working. okay so what I'm thinking is we do a proper sit down lunch, maybe a biryani and some sides, and then tea and desserts after

That's a solid plan — biryani is the right call for a crowd that size because you can make it in big batches and it's a centrepiece dish that everyone likes. For sides, are you thinking classic stuff like salad, samosas, maybe some raita? And who's doing the cooking — your mum, you, split between people?

my mum will do the biryani cos no one makes it like her tbh. I was thinking I could sort the salads and some starters. but I need my siblings to handle dessert and drinks and setting up the house and stuff. the problem is getting them to commit

Okay, here's what I'd suggest — send one clear message in the group chat with a numbered list. Don't ask, assign. Something like:

1. Mum — biryani (sorted)
2. You — salads and starters
3. [Sibling] — dessert (pick up baklawa and basbousa or whatever your family likes)
4. [Sibling] — drinks and cups/plates/napkins
5. [Sibling] — help set up tables and chairs morning of
6. [Sibling] — clean up after

Then say "if you have a problem with your assignment, swap with someone else but everyone needs a job." Direct delegation. If they don't respond in the chat, text them individually.

loool you sound like my mum. she literally says the same thing, 'give them jobs or they'll do nothing'. okay I'm going to do this today. I'm also thinking about getting some decorations for the house, nothing mad but like some nice tablecloths and maybe flowers

That would make a nice difference without being over the top. A simple colour scheme ties it together — gold and green or gold and white are classic for Eid. You could do a nice tablecloth, a small flower arrangement for the table, maybe some fairy lights if you want to go a bit further. Primark and Home Bargains usually have cheap but decent table decor if you don't want to spend loads.

Home Bargains is the one tbh. I'll pop in this weekend. my mum would actually be bare happy if the table looked nice, she always says we don't make enough effort for gatherings anymore. right the other thing is — some of the cousins are bringing kids. do I need to plan activities for them or just let them run around

With 25-30 people in a house, honestly kids will mostly entertain themselves, especially if there are a few of them — they'll form their own little pack. But having one or two small things helps: a colouring station with some Eid-themed printables (loads of free ones online, just print and leave out with crayons), and maybe some party bags with sweets for when they leave. The parents will appreciate it and it takes like 20 minutes to set up. Don't overthink it though — they really just need space and sugar.

space and sugar loool. that's very accurate. okay I think I have a plan now. biryani from mum, I'll do salads and starters, delegate dessert and drinks and setup to the siblings, get some decorations from Home Bargains, and print some colouring sheets for the little ones. yeah?

That's a solid plan. You've got the food covered, the delegation sorted, and the extras handled. One last thing — if you're worried about siblings not following through, set a deadline. Tell them you need confirmation by this Friday or you're reassigning their job to someone else. Nothing motivates like the threat of being publicly swapped out in the family group chat.
the public shame approach loool I love it. right I'm sending the message now before I lose my nerve. Eid mubarak in advance init. thanks for the help
Eid mubarak to you and the family! Hope the group chat responds and the gathering goes brilliantly. Your mum's biryani sounds like the real star of the show.
